extraction.one_line_summary: PARET-PEINTRE Aurélien won from a 7-rider break after 80km of chaos, out-sprinting LEKNESSUND who inherited maglia rosa as Evenepoel's team surrendered it.
tactical_patterns: Soudal Quick-Step deliberately surrendered maglia rosa by allowing 2min+ gap, isolating Evenepoel early to shed podium protocol burden.
80km to form breakaway reveals GC teams unwilling to chase dangerous riders like Leknessund, prioritizing energy preservation over jersey control.
Trek-Segafredo wasted resources with two riders in break (Skujins, Ghebreigzabhier) yet neither could contest finish against fresher attackers.

rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: At 23, absorbed 2min effort to take pink despite losing sprint, showing GC depth beyond pure climbing ability.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: Isolation with 3km remaining suggests Soudal Quick-Step prioritized stage hunting over jersey defense on medium mountain day.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Caught in early split requiring team effort to retrieve, worrying positioning ahead of harder mountain stages.
rider_signals.DE PLUS Laurens: positive: Controlled GC group for Ineos despite no leader present, indicating domestique depth and tactical flexibility.
rider_signals.PARET-PEINTRE Aurélien: positive: French Alps resident proved superior power on Southern Italy hills, winning tactical sprint after bridging to Leknessund.
